scenario: You work as the night caretaker in a small provincial hospital. During one of your rounds you realise that, because of a laboratory accident, some highly toxic fumes are spreading through the ventilation system towards a room in which there are five patients. In another room in the same ward there is just one patient.You activate a switch which allows the toxic fumes to be diverted away from the room. You know that the fumes will be directed to the single room where the patient will die, but the other five will be saved.
Q1_expl: No family values are involved in this scenario.
Q2_expl: The action prioritizes the group of five patients over the single patient.
Q3_expl: No reciprocity is involved in this scenario.
Q4_expl: The action requires bravery and quick thinking to make a difficult decision.
Q5_expl: No monkish virtues are involved in this scenario.
Q6_expl: The action is a difficult moral choice, but prioritizes the greater good.
Q7_expl: No prior possession is involved in this scenario.
moral_expl: The action is morally acceptable due to the greater good being prioritized.
